steps.title: Identify the subject and its structure
steps.content: The subject is the gerund phrase "Having proportionally large populations" — the entire phrase acts as a single, singular noun (like "Running marathons is hard").

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Gerund phrases, regardless of how many words they contain, function as singular subjects and take a singular verb.

A) is — singular present-tense verb; agrees with the singular gerund-phrase subject. Correct.
B) were — plural past tense; both number and tense are wrong. Wrong.
C) are — plural present tense; violates singular subject. Wrong.
D) have been — plural present-perfect; violates singular subject. Wrong.

A is correct.
